meta.scoring: geometric mean of 4 axes: A1 physics gate, A2 pay accuracy, A3 honeypot = 100*(caught/200)^2, A4 curve accuracy (PHIE tol 0.03 / SW 0.10 / PERM 0.5-log at the key's true pay depths)
findings: Structural fixes drove 15.03 -> 22.48: sandstone matrix (un-zeroed A4 on 185 wells), honeypot recall -> 200 (A3 is squared), tighter pay (A2).
A3 honeypot = 100*(caught/200)^2 is the steepest lever; honeypots are geologically normal (decoys live in the curves, not metadata), so recall not precision is the knob.
S2 per-well Rw is best (22.48). S1 proved the SW change is A4-NEUTRAL -> the A4 floor is PHIE and/or PERM, not SW.
M-factorial closed: every gain so far is A2 (pay footage), and LESS footage wins (0.16 beats 0.24). Match-key formulas HURT (M3 ratio 0.98).
A2 is plateauing ~21-22.5. The gap to the leaders (~37) must come from A3 and A4 — A4 is the now-open lever.
meta.next_action: Submit P1 (A4-PHIE read) then C2 (A2-floor read); branch the next window off P1's result.

submissions.label: baseline
submissions.change: dolomite matrix on a clastic field; honeypot threshold 3.0
submissions.read: Root cause of the low score: dolomite matrix zeroed A4 on 185 wells; honeypot recall far below 200.

submissions.label: hard135
submissions.change: sandstone matrix + raw-OOB honeypot detector (61->135) + tighter pay

submissions.label: top200
submissions.read: +3.40, biggest single gain. Confirms A3 (squared) dominates. Factorial anchor.

submissions.label: Rw decoupled probe
submissions.change: per-well Rw on the OUTPUT SW only; PAY_FLAG frozen = iter3
submissions.read: Ratio ~1.00 -> the SW change is A4-NEUTRAL. SW is not the binding A4 curve.

submissions.label: Rw per-well (coupled)
submissions.change: per-well Rw drives SW and pay (footage 0.24 -> 0.16)
submissions.read: +1.26 ratio. A4 is flat (S1), so the whole gain is A2: cutting pay overprediction. New baseline B'.

submissions.label: match-key full
submissions.read: Ratio 1.05. Below S2 -> the documented key does not beat per-well Rw.

submissions.label: match-key pay only
submissions.change: key pay cutoffs only (VSH<0.40, PHIE>0.06, SW<0.60, no PERM)
submissions.read: Ratio 1.12 -> key pay cutoffs help A2 (Jaccard up).

submissions.label: match-key formulas
submissions.read: Ratio 0.98 -> key formulas HURT curve accuracy. Drop.

submissions.label: phie density
submissions.change: written PHIE from density porosity only (drop neutron averaging); pay/SW frozen on B'

rules.then: Density porosity matches the key at pay depths -> PHIE WAS the A4 floor (neutron gas-suppression). Adopt density-out; sweep rms + matrix/fluid const next. Path to the leaders.
A4-neutral, like SW in S1. PHIE is not the floor -> pivot to the PERM (Timur) probe.
Effective PHIE was right; density-out is worse. Revert.

submissions.label: Rw + key pay + low footage
submissions.change: per-well Rw + key pay cutoffs + tighter SW (footage 0.099)

rules.then: Footage optimum is below 0.16 -> push lower next window.
0.10-0.16 plateau -> A2 is done, stop cutting footage.
Overcut (dropped real pay). Floor ~0.16; C1's 0.146 is near optimum.

submissions.label: Rw + key pay
submissions.read: Held: interpolated by C2. Submit only if C2's read is ambiguous.
